points.text: Erasca, Inc. (ERAS) stock fell 48% ($9.25) following disclosures of a patient death and intellectual property disputes regarding its pan-RAS molecular glue candidate ERAS-0015.
Revolution Medicines accused Erasca of patent infringement and trade secret misappropriation concerning the pan-RAS molecular glue ERAS-0015, triggering a securities class action.
Erasca Inc. (ERAS) stock declined 48% ($9.25) following disclosure of a patient death and intellectual property dispute regarding its pan-RAS molecular glue candidate ERAS-0015.

Revolution Medicines accused Erasca of patent infringement and trade secret misappropriation concerning the pan-RAS molecular glue ERAS-0015, challenging Erasca's 'best-in-class' claims against RMC-6236.
Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — MTX240 — Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumors (GIST) — Molecular glue inducing PDE3a/SLFN12 complex formation to drive RNase-mediated apoptosis independent of KIT signaling.

Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — MTX240 — Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumors (GIST) — Molecular glue inducing PDE3a/SLFN12 complex formation to drive RNase-mediated apoptosis independent of KIT signaling; Health Canada approved expansion of registrational Phase 3 trial in Familial Adenomatous Polyposis.

Erasca Inc. — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutated solid tumors — Pan-RAS molecular glue; preliminary Phase I data reported April 2026 included one fatality at 24 mg dose; stock fell 48% following disclosure of patent infringement allegations by Revolution Medicines and limitations in cross-study comparisons.

Erasca, Inc. (ERAS) faces a securities class action following a $2.8 billion market cap loss after Revolution Medicines accused the company of patent infringement regarding its pan-RAS molecular glue ERAS-0015.

Erasca's ERAS-0015, an investigational oral pan-RAS molecular glue for RAS-mutant solid tumors including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, is under scrutiny after a patient death occurred one month post-treatment.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics (NASDAQ: GLUE) — Clinical-stage biotechnology company — Molecular glue degrader (MGD) medicines — Announced inducement equity grants to four new non-executive employees to support development of highly selective MGDs for autoimmune, inflammatory, and oncology indications.

Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — MTX240 — Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumors (GIST) — Molecular glue mechanism identified; induces proximity between PDE3a and SLFN12 proteins to drive RNase-mediated apoptosis independent of KIT signaling.

Erasca — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Pan-RAS molecular glue; patient death reported one month post-dosing; patent infringement lawsuit filed by Revolution Medicines alleging trade secret misappropriation.

Erasca — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Pan-RAS molecular glue candidate; patient death reported one month post-treatment; intellectual property validity challenged by Revolution Medicines.
Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — MTX240 — Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumors (GIST) — Molecular glue mechanism stabilizes SLFN12 to induce apoptosis in GIST cells co-expressing PDE3a and SLFN12.

Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — MTX240 — Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumors — Molecular glue targeting PDE3a/SLFN12 complex; Health Canada granted approval to expand registrational Phase 3 trial to include Familial Adenomatous Polyposis indication.

Erasca Inc. — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutated solid tumors — Pan-RAS molecular glue; April 2026 preliminary Phase I data revealed one patient death at 24 mg dose; company faces class action lawsuit alleging misleading statements regarding patent infringement risks and data comparability vs Revolution Medicines.

Erasca — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors — Securities class action filed after Revolution Medicines accused Erasca of patent infringement and trade secret misappropriation concerning its pan-RAS molecular glue; stock dropped 48% ($9.25) on disclosure of patient death and IP challenges.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Molecular glue degrader (MGD) pipeline — Autoimmune, inflammatory, and oncology diseases — Granted inducement equity awards (20,050 stock options at $19.67/share; 4,400 RSUs) to four new employees under 2026 Inducement Plan to advance three clinical-stage MGD programs.

Erasca — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Patient death reported one month post-treatment; Revolution Medicines issued legal challenge alleging patent infringement and trade secret misappropriation regarding pan-RAS molecular glue mechanism.

Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — MTX240 — Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumors (GIST) — Molecular glue candidate stabilizes SLFN12 by bringing PDE3a and SLFN12 into close proximity; mechanism independent of KIT signaling.

Erasca — Pan-RAS molecular glue development — ERAS-0015 — Facing $2.8B market cap loss, securities class action, and IP challenges from Revolution Medicines regarding trade secrets and improper dose comparisons.

Revolution Medicines — Intellectual property enforcement — RMC-6236 (competitor to ERAS-0015) — Accused Erasca of misappropriating trade secrets and making improper clinical comparisons between its RMC-6236 and Erasca's ERAS-0015.
Erasca — Pan-RAS molecular glue development — ERAS-0015 — Facing securities class action and IP litigation from Revolution Medicines; market cap loss of $2.8 billion reported.

Revolution Medicines — Pan-RAS molecular glue IP enforcement — RMC-6236 (comparator) — Accused Erasca of misappropriating trade secrets and making improper dose comparisons between RMC-6236 and ERAS-0015.

Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — Clinical-stage pipeline advancement — MTX240 (molecular glue) + eRapa + tolimidone — Raised $3.5M via registered direct offering and private placement; MTX240 targets GIST via PDE3a/SLFN12 proximity induction.
Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — Molecular glue development for GIST — MTX240 — Mechanism: PDE3a/SLFN12 proximity induction; Status: Phase 3 registrational trial expanded in FAP per Health Canada approval.

Erasca Inc. — Pan-RAS molecular glue commercialization — ERAS-0015 — Status: Phase I ongoing; facing class action lawsuit over alleged patent infringement by Revolution Medicines and disclosure of 24 mg dose fatality.

Erasca — Pan-RAS molecular glue development — ERAS-0015 — Facing $2.8B market cap loss and legal action after patient death and Revolution Medicines IP dispute; previously touted as 'best-in-class' oral therapy.

Revolution Medicines — Intellectual property enforcement — RMC-6236 (competitor to ERAS-0015) — Accused Erasca of misappropriating trade secrets and making improper dose comparisons between its RMC-6236 and Erasca's ERAS-0015.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Development of first-in-class and only-in-class molecular glue degraders — QuEEN™ discovery engine (AI-guided chemistry, structural biology, proteomics) — Three clinical-stage programs in autoimmune, inflammatory, and oncology indications; ongoing collaborations with leading pharma companies.

Erasca — Pan-RAS molecular glue development — ERAS-0015 — Facing $2.8 billion market cap loss, securities class action, and IP litigation from Revolution Medicines over alleged improper comparison to RMC-6236 and trade secret theft.

Revolution Medicines — Pan-RAS molecular glue IP protection — RMC-6236 — Asserted patent infringement claims against Erasca's ERAS-0015, alleging Erasca misappropriated trade secrets and made deceptive dose comparisons (40mg vs 400mg).

Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — Molecular glue pipeline development — MTX240 — Clinical stage biopharmaceutical company advancing MTX240 as a molecular glue for GIST; raised $3.5M via registered direct offering and private placement in June 2026.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: Investigational — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Comparator: RMC-6236 (alleged improper comparison of 40mg vs 400mg cohorts) — Safety: One patient death reported one month after treatment.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: Investigational — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Comparator: RMC-6236 (400 mg) — Adverse Event: Patient death reported one month after receiving 40 mg dose cohort.

MTX240 — Mechanism: Molecular glue bringing PDE3a and SLFN12 into close proximity to form stable complex — Phase: Clinical stage — Population: Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumors (GIST) cells co-expressing PDE3a/SLFN12 — Comparator: None stated — Endpoints/Results: Stabilizes SLFN12 to drive RNase-mediated apoptosis independent of KIT signaling.

MTX240 — Mechanism: Molecular glue stabilizing PDE3a/SLFN12 complex to induce RNase-mediated apoptosis — Phase: 3 — Population: GIST and FAP — Comparator: None stated — Results: Independent of KIT signaling; trial expansion approved by Health Canada.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: 1 — Population: RAS-mutated solid tumors — Comparator: Cross-study analyses vs Revolution Medicines candidates (alleged) — Endpoints/Results: One fatality reported at 24 mg dose; data limitations cited in class action complaint.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: Investigational — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Comparator: RMC-6236 (alleged improper comparison) — Safety: One patient death reported one month post-treatment; adverse event led to ER visit.

Monte Rosa MGDs — Mechanism: Small molecule protein degraders targeting neosubstrates with unprecedented selectivity via QuEEN™ platform — Phase: Clinical (three programs) — Population: Patients with serious autoimmune, inflammatory, and oncology diseases — Differentiation: Treats diseases inaccessible to other modalities including other degraders.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: Investigational — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Comparator: RMC-6236 (alleged improper 40mg vs 400mg dose comparison) — Safety: One patient death reported one month after treatment.

MTX240 — Mechanism: Molecular glue inducing PDE3a-SLFN12 complex formation — Phase: Clinical stage — Population: GIST cancer cells co-expressing PDE3a and SLFN12 — Comparator: None specified — Endpoints/Results: Stabilizes SLFN12 to drive RNase-mediated apoptosis independent of KIT signaling.

Erasca — Securities Class Action — ERAS-0015 — Stock dropped 48% ($9.25) to a $2.8B market cap loss; Hagens Berman filing covers period Jan 14, 2025–Apr 26, 2026; IP dispute with Revolution Medicines over in-licensed Joyo patent family.

Erasca — Securities Class Action — ERAS-0015 — Stock dropped 48% ($9.25) to a $2.8 billion market cap loss following IP dispute with Revolution Medicines and patient death disclosure; class period Jan 14, 2025–Apr 26, 2026.

Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — Capital raise — $3.5M total ($0.8M registered offering, $1.0M private placement, $1.7M warrant inducement) — Proceeds to fund clinical programs including MTX240 molecular glue for GIST.

Erasca Inc. — Class action lawsuit filed by Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP — ERAS-0015 — Allegations: Misleading statements on patent infringement risks (Revolution Medicines) and data comparability; stock dropped 48% post-April 27, 2026 disclosures.

Erasca — Intellectual property dispute — ERAS-0015 — Received legal challenge from Revolution Medicines regarding patent validity and trade secret misappropriation; previously claimed in-licensing of one patent family from Joyo including one issued US patent.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Equity compensation — Inducement grants under Nasdaq Rule 5635(c)(4) — Granted 20,050 non-qualified stock options (exercise price $19.67) and 4,400 restricted stock units to four new non-executive employees; awards vest over 4 years.

Erasca — Securities class action litigation — ERAS-0015 — Stock dropped 48% ($9.25) to $2.8 billion market cap loss following disclosure of patient death and Revolution Medicines IP lawsuit; class period Jan 14, 2025–Apr 26, 2026.
